name: Validations

unions.name: ValidationResult
unions.comment: A Validation result behaves very similarly to a Result, the main difference is that you can have an aggregation of all the errors instead of a single one.

values.name: beginValidation
values.comment:
Wraps value in a ValidationResult.

    (beginValidation {password = "somepass"}) == Valid {password = "somepass"}

values.name: equals
values.comment:
Validates equality between two values.

    (beginValidation {password = "somepass"})
    |> equals 8 8 "Values are not the same" == Err {password = "somepass} ["Values are not the same"]
values.type: a -> a -> String -> ValidationResult b -> ValidationResult b

values.name: validateLengthOf
values.comment:
Validates the length of a string value.

    (beginValidation {password = "somepass"})
    |> validateLengthOf .password 8 "Password is too short" == Err {password = "somepass} ["Password is too short"]
values.type: (a -> String) -> Int -> String -> ValidationResult a -> ValidationResult a

values.name: validateMatchOf
values.comment:
Validates a regex match of a value.

    (beginValidation {password = "somepass"})
    |> validateMatchOf .password (regex "somepass") "Not in regex" == Err {password = "somepass} ["Not in regex"]
values.type: (a -> String) -> Regex -> String -> ValidationResult a -> ValidationResult a

values.name: validatePresenceOf
values.comment:
Validates the presence of a string value.

    (beginValidation {password = "somepass"})
    |> validatePresenceOf .password "Password is too short"
values.type: (a -> String) -> String -> ValidationResult a -> ValidationResult a
